_comment_top: R6 Wave 8 — seed row for the LegalResearchHandler 'LookupCase' method. Replaces the second of the two legacy hardcoded LLM functions previously registered on LegalResearchTools (removed from SprkChatAgentFactory.ResolveTools lines ~1107-1165).
_comment_routing: Runtime invocation routes to the C# class via sprk_handlerclass = 'LegalResearchHandler'. The handler reads sprk_configuration.method = 'LookupCase' to dispatch to the citation-anchored Bing Grounding path (search query is wrapped with site: filters for law.justia.com, courtlistener.com, scholar.google.com).
_comment_method_dispatch: Two rows (LEGAL-RESEARCH / LEGAL-CASE-LOOKUP) share one handler class — same pattern as TextRefinementHandler (Wave 7 Q9) and KnowledgeRetrievalHandler (Wave 7c). Each row's sprk_configuration.method discriminator selects the internal method.
_comment_security: sprk_requiredcapability = 'legal_research' (= PlaybookCapabilities.LegalResearch, Dataverse option set integer 100000007) gates this tool to playbooks whose capability set includes 'legal_research'. Standalone chat (capabilities = CoreCapabilities; 'legal_research' NOT included) cannot reach this handler — preserving the pre-Wave-8 security boundary.
_comment_adr_015: ADR-015 binding: case citations may carry surrounding PII (e.g., 'My client's case: 123 F.3d 456'). The handler invokes QuerySanitizer.Sanitize() BEFORE every Bing Grounding call — the case citation itself is preserved (it IS the search target) but client-name prefixes and matter references are stripped. Telemetry logs citation LENGTH + result COUNT + duration only.
_comment_adr_018: ADR-018 binding: kill switch via BingGroundingOptions.Enabled is checked BEFORE any network call. When disabled, the handler returns a successful ToolResult with a user-readable degradation message directing the user to Westlaw / LexisNexis.
_comment_description_loadbearing: sprk_description is LOAD-BEARING per NFR-12: the LLM uses the description text to decide WHEN to invoke this tool. The exact phrasing below is preserved verbatim from the legacy hardcoded registration so the LLM's tool-selection behavior is unchanged after migration.
_comment_consumption: Read by scripts/Seed-TypedHandlers.ps1. Idempotent — script queries by sprk_handlerclass + sprk_toolcode + sprk_name 'SYS-%' filter; UPSERTs.
sprk_description: Look up a specific legal case by its standard citation (e.g., 123 F.3d 456 (9th Cir. 2020)). Returns the case holding and a source URL from an authoritative legal database.
_comment_availableincontexts: 100000001 = Chat. Chat-only matches the pre-R6 hardcoded registration; no playbook node executor exists for legal_research per NFR-08.

sprk_jsonschema.title: LegalResearchHandler.LookupCase Request
sprk_jsonschema.description: Argument shape for chat-driven invocation of the LookupCase method. The 'citation' is a specific legal case citation; the handler sanitizes surrounding PII, anchors the query to authoritative legal databases, and returns the case holding plus a source URL.

citation.description: Case citation in standard legal format (e.g., '123 F.3d 456 (9th Cir. 2020)' or 'Roe v. Wade, 410 U.S. 113 (1973)'). Case names are acceptable as they are public record; client-name prefixes and matter references are stripped by the sanitizer before the query is forwarded to Bing.

sprk_configuration._comment: method = 'LookupCase' is the dispatch discriminator. This row exposes only the citation-anchored Bing Grounding method.
